articles.title: Could Guardiola's best and Arteta's worst month decide title?
articles.summary: As the Premier League season approaches its finale, April could be the defining month for leaders Arsenal and closest challengers Manchester City. BBC Sport looks at the stats to explain why.

articles.title: Premier League darts: Littler faces Price in Rotterdam with 6pm start
articles.summary: The latest fixtures and results from the 2026 Premier League Darts, where world No 1 Luke Littler and defending champion Luke Humphries are among those in action.

articles.title: Great Britain stun Australia to reach Billie Jean King Cup Finals
articles.summary: Jodie Burrage and Harriet Dart sealed a 3-0 victory for Great Britain in their Billie Jean King Cup qualifier against Australia as they won their doubles match in straight sets.

articles.title: West Ham have fresh hope - but Spurs now in uncharted territory
articles.summary: West Ham's thumping win over Wolves not only boosts their own Premier League survival hopes, but plunges Tottenham into the drop zone - and to new depths.

articles.title: De Zerbi 'not surprised' by reaction to Greenwood comments
articles.summary: Tottenham manager Roberto de Zerbi says he was "not surprised" by the reaction from supporters over comments he made about Mason Greenwood.

articles.title: Fernandez sorry but will miss Man City - Rosenior
articles.summary: Chelsea midfielder Enzo Fernandez is still unavailable for the upcoming home match against Manchester City despite a recent apology, head coach Liam Rosenior says.

articles.title: EFL preview: Open-top buses booked as finish line comes into view
articles.summary: Promotions within grasp, relegation six-pointers - here are five things to look out for in the English Football League this weekend.

articles.title: Is Fernandes' own goal the worst of all time?
articles.summary: Porto's Martim Fernandes scored a bizarre own goal for Porto against Nottingham Forest on Thursday, but how does it compare to the worst of all-time?

articles.title: I feel complete backing from owners and fans - Slot
articles.summary: Liverpool manager Arne Slot insists he feels "complete support" from the club's hierarchy and fans despite a run of poor on-field performances and growing discontent about his future as manager

articles.title: Spurs lead race for Robertson - Saturday's gossip
articles.summary: Tottenham in pole position to sign Andy Robertson, Manchester City in talks for Morten Hjulmand, Arsenal monitoring Anthony Gordon and Kees Smit attracting Premier League interest.

articles.title: We asked fans of Premier League clubs how they are feeling - find out the results
articles.summary: As the Premier League returns after a three-week hiatus, we asked fans how they are feeling about their club.

articles.title: West Ham thrash Wolves to move out of relegation zone
articles.summary: Taty Castellanos and Konstantinos Mavropanos both score twice as West Ham beat Wolves to move out of the relegation zone in the Premier League.

articles.title: Augsburg miss late penalty in tense  Hoffenheim draw
articles.summary: Augsburg's Alexis Claude-Maurice misses a late penalty in an eventful draw with Champions League hopefuls Hoffenheim.

articles.title: 'It will be smiles' - Nuno on moving out of relegation zone
articles.summary: West Ham boss Nuno Espirito Santo is "delighted" with Konstantinos Mavropanos, as his side thrash Wolves 4-0 to move out of the Premier League relegation zone.

articles.title: What's next in captivating Scottish Premiership title race?
articles.summary: Hearts host Motherwell, Celtic welcome St Mirren and Rangers travel to Falkirk in the latest instalment of the fascinating Scottish Premiership title race.

articles.title: Watch Connolly goal as Queen's Park add to Ross County misery
articles.summary: Watch Aidan Connolly's first-half headed goal that was enough to give Queen's Park a 1-0 win over Ross County to move up to fifth in the Scottish Championship and leave the visitors two points adrift ...

articles.title: Coburn misses sitter as Millwall settle for draw at West Brom
articles.summary: Josh Coburn will rue missing his first-half sitter as Millwall were forced to settle for a goalless draw at an impressive West Brom who themselves took a major stride towards Championship survival.
